description: Fix two race conditions in the browser extension: (1) confirmation badge double-decrement when both background and side panel timeouts fire for the same confirmation, and (2) missed cleanup on service worker wake when ws:state arrives before restoreWsConnectedState completes.

userStories.title: Fix confirmation badge double-decrement race
userStories.description: As a user, I want the confirmation badge count to be accurate so that I know how many confirmations are actually pending.
acceptanceCriteria: clearConfirmationBadge is idempotent per confirmation id — calling it twice with the same id decrements the count only once
When the background timeout fires and the side panel also sends sp:confirmationTimeout for the same id, pendingConfirmationCount decrements by exactly 1 total
When 3 confirmations are pending and 1 is resolved via both paths, badge shows 2 (not 1)
Existing unit tests for confirmation-badge continue to pass
userStories.notes:
Bug location: platform/browser-extension/src/confirmation-badge.ts

The issue: clearConfirmationBadge(id) at line 85-91 always decrements pendingConfirmationCount regardless of whether this id was already cleared. When both the background timeout (line 63-66) and the side panel timeout (handleSpConfirmationTimeout in background-message-handlers.ts:184-191) fire for the same confirmation id, the count decrements twice.

The background timeout callback calls clearConfirmationBadge(id) which decrements count. Later, when the side panel sends sp:confirmationTimeout, handleSpConfirmationTimeout calls clearConfirmationBackgroundTimeout(id) (no-op since timeout already fired and was deleted from the map) then calls clearConfirmationBadge(id) which decrements again.

Fix: Track which confirmation ids have been cleared. In clearConfirmationBadge, check if the id was already cleared before decrementing. A simple Set<string> of cleared ids works. When a new confirmation arrives with the same id (checked in notifyConfirmationRequest), remove it from the cleared set. Alternatively, only decrement if the id is present in the confirmationTimeouts map or a separate pending set — the key insight is to make the decrement idempotent per id.

Simplest approach: add a Set<string> called `clearedConfirmationIds`. In clearConfirmationBadge, if id is defined and already in the set, skip the decrement. Otherwise add to set and decrement. In notifyConfirmationRequest, remove from clearedConfirmationIds when a new confirmation with that id arrives. In clearAllConfirmationBadges, clear the set.

userStories.title: Fix restoreWsConnectedState race on service worker wake
userStories.description: As a developer, I want WebSocket disconnect cleanup to always run so that stale tab state cache and confirmation badges are cleared after service worker suspension.
acceptanceCriteria: When a ws:state connected=false message arrives before restoreWsConnectedState completes, clearTabStateCache and clearAllConfirmationBadges are still called
The wasConnected vs nowConnected transition detection accounts for the async restore not having completed yet
No regression when ws:state connected=true arrives (normal connect flow still works)
userStories.notes:
Bug location: platform/browser-extension/src/background-message-handlers.ts

The issue: restoreWsConnectedState (line 27-38) reads wsConnected from chrome.storage.session asynchronously via .then(). Meanwhile, wsConnected defaults to false (line 22). If a ws:state connected=false message arrives from the offscreen document before the .then() callback executes, handleWsState (line 71-88) sees wasConnected=false and nowConnected=false. The guard at line 83 `if (!nowConnected && wasConnected)` is false, so clearTabStateCache() and clearAllConfirmationBadges() are never called.

This matters after MV3 service worker suspension: the service worker was suspended while connected (wsConnected=true was in storage.session). On wake, restoreWsConnectedState starts reading storage. If the offscreen document detects a disconnect and sends ws:state connected=false before the storage read completes, the cleanup is skipped.

Fix: In handleWsState, always call clearTabStateCache and clearAllConfirmationBadges on disconnect (when nowConnected=false), regardless of wasConnected. The cleanup functions are idempotent — calling them when already in a clean state is harmless. Change line 83 from `if (!nowConnected && wasConnected)` to `if (!nowConnected)`. This ensures disconnect cleanup always runs, even if the previous state is unknown due to the async restore race.

Alternative: make restoreWsConnectedState synchronous by awaiting it before registering the message listener. But this would require restructuring initBackgroundMessageHandlers to be async, which is a larger change. The simpler fix (always clean up on disconnect) is safer.
